description_long: This object is a rugged, rectangular handheld electronic device resembling an old-fashioned two-way radio or walkie-talkie. Its exterior is made of a dark, marbled material with visible scratches and wear, suggesting heavy use or durability for outdoor environments. The front features a large, cracked display screen above a numeric keypad with raised, tactile buttons, and several additional function keys. On the top, there are two cylindrical protrusions, likely an antenna and a control knob, along with a smaller pink button. The left side has a prominent rotary knob and a blue port or slot near the bottom, while the right side is mostly flat. The back is plain, with the same marbled texture as the rest of the body.
description: A rugged, handheld electronic device with a cracked screen, numeric keypad, and antenna, resembling a walkie-talkie or portable radio.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cracked display, full numeric keypad, and function buttons.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the rotary knob and blue port near the bottom.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain marbled surface and top-mounted controls.
description_view_3: Right side view showing a flat surface with no visible controls.
